purpose: This policy describes QC for virology testing (viral culture, antigen detection, molecular viral testing, serology). Required by 42 CFR 493.1256 (controls), 42 CFR 493.1261-1278 (specialty QC).

cfr_text_blocks.label: Standard: Controls in each run
cfr_text_blocks.verbatim: Include the following controls in each run of patient specimens: (i) At least two control materials at different concentrations for each quantitative procedure. (ii) A negative control material and a positive control material for each qualitative procedure. (iii) A negative control material and a control material with graded or titered reactivity, respectively, for each test producing graded or titered results.

policy_statements: Viral culture: positive and negative controls run per the procedure cadence; cell line viability and morphology assessed per QC plan.
Antigen detection assays: positive and negative controls each run; internal control read where the kit supports.
Molecular viral assays: positive, negative, and internal amplification controls each run per the Molecular QC policy.
Serology assays: positive and negative controls each run; quantitative serology (titer or index) uses two-level controls per 42 CFR 493.1256(d)(2)(i).
Failed controls trigger run rejection and corrective action before patient results are released.
procedure_steps: Run setup includes the specialty-specific required controls.
Control results are interpreted alongside patient results; failed controls trigger investigation and re-run.
Monthly review by the medical director or designee per the Quality Control Plan policy.
definitions: Confirmation that the cell line used for viral culture is viable and shows expected morphology, performed on a defined cadence.
